summary.intent: Implement a Nostr DM channel for Moltis, bringing it to parity with existing channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) including inbound/outbound DMs, OTP pairing flow, web UI, docs, and tests.
summary.outcome: Full Nostr channel implementation shipped across 12+ commits: new `crates/nostr/` crate (~1700 LOC), web UI add/edit/onboarding flows, E2E tests, documentation, metrics instrumentation, NIP-44 fallback decryption, and real integration tests against live relays. All 274 E2E tests passing except one validation test that was fixed in the final commit.

repo: Channel plugins follow a strict pattern: optional dep in gateway/httpd/cli Cargo.toml with feature flag, registered under #[cfg(feature = "nostr")] in server.rs, ChannelType variant with as_str/display_name/secret_fields/classify_chat/descriptor methods
ChannelType::ALL constant and descriptor() match arm must be updated when adding new variants; there is a test that checks ALL variant count

AccountStateMap must use std::sync::RwLock (not tokio::sync::RwLock) — sync ChannelPlugin trait methods are called from async context, causing 'Cannot block the current thread from within a runtime' panic with tokio locks
OTP flow requires: check for pending OTP *before* the access gate runs, decrypt first, then verify the code — not after access control blocks the sender
nostr-sdk prelude imports both nip04 and nip44 with glob, causing name collision on `decrypt` — must use fully-qualified nostr_sdk::nips::nip44::decrypt path
nostr-sdk nip04 and nip44 features are NOT in default — must explicitly enable in workspace Cargo.toml
Config ChannelsConfig uses #[serde(flatten)] + extra HashMap for unknown channel types; named fields (telegram, discord, etc.) take priority — adding nostr as named field is the right approach

Web channel error divs use class='channel-error block' but CSS .channel-error has display:none which overrides Tailwind block — conditional render handles visibility, so channel-error class is counterproductive on conditionally-rendered elements
nostr-sdk broadcast::Receiver from notifications() must be obtained BEFORE connect()+subscribe() to avoid missing early events
Website index files already had Nostr references across all 10 locales — only the SVG icon file was missing

code.finding: blocking_read()/blocking_write() on tokio::sync::RwLock panics from within tokio runtime — AccountStateMap must use std::sync::RwLock
Collapsible match lint: nested if let RelayMessage::Event inside match RelayPoolNotification::Message should be collapsed into single match arm with && pattern
UTF-8 boundary safe truncation: use str::floor_char_boundary(MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES) instead of &plaintext[..MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES] to avoid panic on multi-byte chars
ALL constant and descriptor() match must both be updated when adding ChannelType variants; test checks ALL.len() == discriminant count
len_without_is_empty clippy lint: if a struct has len() it must also implement is_empty()
Biome formatter requires chained .split().map().filter() to be on separate lines, not a single line
channels.offered default list controls what appears in web UI channel picker — must include 'nostr'

workflow: nostr-sdk notifications() broadcast receiver must be subscribed before connect()/subscribe() calls to not miss EOSE and early events
taplo fmt reformats TOML files and changes key ordering — creates noise in diffs for unrelated crates (msteams, secret-store, webhooks)
cargo +nightly fmt --all is used for Rust formatting; biome for JS — both must pass in CI
When cherry-picking onto main after a rebase, use git cherry-pick range syntax (base_sha..tip_sha) to replay all commits cleanly
gh secret set is used to add CI secrets: gh secret set KEY_NAME --body 'value'
Greptile automated PR review adds inline comments that must be addressed — run greploop to iterate until 0 remaining comments
std::sync::RwLockReadGuard is not Send, so cannot be held across .await points — extract needed values into locals before any await

friction: nostr-sdk API surface required multiple compile-attempt cycles to discover: nip04/nip44 not in default features, subscribe() takes single Filter not Vec, disconnect() returns () not Result, notifications() deduplicates events, to_bech32() returns Result<String, Infallible> not String
tokio::sync::RwLock vs std::sync::RwLock caused a runtime panic that crashed all 274 E2E tests — not caught by unit tests since tests don't exercise the full server startup path
Main branch used unrelated histories (squashed base), making git merge require --allow-unrelated-histories and creating 70+ conflicts; had to abort and cherry-pick instead
CSS .channel-error { display: none } fighting Tailwind block class caused E2E test failure — the class is semantically wrong for conditionally-rendered elements
nostr prelude glob imports both nip04 and nip44 decrypt functions causing name collision — required fully-qualified paths
OTP flow was wired but broken (challenge emitted but code never sent, verification never reachable) — required PR review to catch
open_items: NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs (more privacy, less widely supported) — deferred as future enhancement
Metrics counters/histograms are defined but not all code paths are instrumented (initial implementation)
Integration tests use #[ignore] and require NOSTR_TEST_BOT_KEY / NOSTR_TEST_SENDER_KEY env vars — need to be added to GitHub Actions provider-integration workflow
Contract test suite (moltis_channels::contract) not run against NostrPlugin — requires valid credentials to start, unlike TestPlugin
